hard_rules.message: Frame oil as cosmetic finish, friction support, or pre-wash length protection, not water-like moisture or medical scalp treatment. Assume finishing oil by default for broad oil use unless the user says scalp, pre-wash, massage, einwirken, growth, or hair-loss language.
Do not promise regrowth, thicker hair, structural repair, split-end repair, color protection, or medical scalp treatment from oil.

Do not recommend neat essential-oil application, dilution recipes, drop counts, DIY scalp-treatment protocols, or essential-oil routines for scalp symptoms; do not present rosemary, peppermint, massage, castor, coconut, or carrier oils as substitutes for clinical hair-loss evaluation or treatment.

General questions about whether rosemary oil, castor oil, scalp massage, or oil helps growth should be handled as non-product general_advice with care_category oil, no unasked product cards, and conservative evidence wording unless active hair-loss or scalp symptoms trigger safety_boundary.
Do not collapse pre-wash oils, silicone finishing serums, masks, medicated dandruff products, and scalp treatments into the same recommendation bucket.

Do not infer oil role, heat protection, color protection, scalp suitability, ingredient effects, fragrance, silicone-free status, oil-free status, exact cadence, or exact protocol from product names, brand lines, or category prose.

Plain oil is not a heat protectant without product-specific support; unless selected product metadata has an explicit supported heat-protection claim, recommend a real heat protectant before heat, meaning a product with explicit heat-protectant claim before heat, and keep oil only as finish/after styling or pre-wash length protection where appropriate.

For named oil, serum, ingredient, or product-specific claim checks, use product catalog data or select_products before stating role, heat protection, color protection, scalp suitability, ingredient effects, fragrance, silicone-free status, oil-free status, exact cadence, or exact protocol; for a single named product detail check, keep requested_product_count 1 and count_policy exact aligned between select_products and the terminal request_interpretation.

Placement/order questions such as 'Kommt Oel vor oder nach Leave-in?' are routine_explanation with routine_intent none unless the user asks to add, remove, replace, or change saved/current routine state. Do not call build_or_fix_routine, return routine payloads, or include routine_step_ids for ordering-only questions; saved/current routine changes require build_or_fix_routine.

When the user asks 'Maske oder Oel?' after dry/frizzy lengths and a lightweight routine goal, do not make oil the main care add-on; prefer a light occasional mask and keep oil only as a tiny finish if needed.

When sudden shedding, patchy loss, severe or persistent thinning, burning, pain, pustules, wounds, persistent flakes, inflamed scalp, or medically adjacent symptoms dominate, use safety_boundary with care_category none and do not escalate oiling.

soft_rubrics.message: When the user asks whether oil is useful, needed, or a fit, use supported general_advice as a category-fit assessment: decide whether oil is the right lever, optional, too heavy, or weaker than another category without showing unasked product cards.

Clarify whether the user wants finish/tips use, pre-wash length protection, cautious scalp comfort, or growth support before selecting products; assume finishing oil by default for broad oil use unless the user says scalp, pre-wash, massage, einwirken, growth, hair-loss language, or clearly asks about protecting lengths before shampoo.

For finish versus pre-wash education, anchor the distinction to user concerns and goals: finishing oil for shine, surface frizz, smoother tips, and after-styling polish; pre-wash length protection for dry, porous, bleached, colored, or heat-stressed lengths before shampoo, not scalp by default.
For fine, oily, flat, low-density, coated, or buildup-prone hair, keep dose tiny, end-focused, pre-wash-only, or skip oil if another layer is likely to weigh hair down.
For pre-wash oiling, include correct wash-out: apply before washing, leave on briefly, emulsify shampoo into oil before adding much water, rinse, then condition.
Separate pre-wash oils, finishing serums, masks, medicated scalp products, and scalp comfort oiling before making a recommendation.

For oil-versus-leave-in, mask, conditioner, bondbuilder, heat protectant, or deep cleansing questions, answer as balanced category_education with care_category none unless one category becomes primary; load multiple guidance packages when boundaries matter.

Use count_policy exact for explicit counts and default when no count is stated; exact means exactly requested only when enough grounded, suitable, available products exist, otherwise return fewer and explain the fit or availability constraint.

For saved/current routine changes, map add or change wording to routine_intent modify, remove wording to remove_step, and replacing one product with another to replace_product; only claim saved changes after routine tooling confirms them.

required_grounding.when: Concrete oil names, category membership, recommendation order, finish/pre-wash role, weight, fit, availability, or product claims are mentioned.

Named oil, serum, ingredient, heat claim, scalp suitability, fragrance, color protection, silicone-free/oil-free status, exact cadence, or product-specific oil claim is checked; keep count fields aligned as 1 exact for a single named-product detail check.
The user asks to add, remove, replace, or change oil in a saved/current routine.

ask_when.condition: It is unclear whether the user wants finish, pre-wash length protection, scalp comfort, or growth support.
ask_when.question_policy: Ask one purpose-first follow-up if the answer would change; otherwise default dry-ends language to a tiny end-focused finish and general growth-claim language to conservative non-product general_advice.

ask_when.condition: Scalp oiling is requested and symptoms could be irritated, persistent, painful, flaky, pustular, patchy-loss, or medical.
ask_when.question_policy: Ask one safety follow-up or route conservatively away from routine-style oiling.

ask_when.condition: The user asks whether rosemary oil, castor oil, scalp massage, or oil helps growth without naming symptoms.
ask_when.question_policy: Do not ask for product preferences or show product cards; answer the growth-claim boundary conservatively unless the wording suggests sudden shedding, patchy loss, pain, burning, pustules, wounds, persistent flakes, or another safety signal.

ask_when.condition: Fine, low-density, oily-root, flat, coated, or buildup-prone hair may make oil a poor fit.
ask_when.question_policy: Ask one follow-up only if the distinction changes whether oil should be finish-only, pre-wash-only, or skipped.

ask_when.condition: A product_detail question lacks the exact oil, serum, ingredient, or claim.
ask_when.question_policy: Ask one concise follow-up for the exact product or ingredient if product-specific role, heat protection, scalp suitability, fragrance, color protection, silicone-free/oil-free status, cadence, or protocol would materially change the answer.
